tool.name: runget
tool.summary: Report a normalized public-run manifest for one accession-backed archive run

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `runget` is the manifest-oriented public-run companion to `runinfo`. It resolves one provider-qualified archive run accession through the governed archive-provider seam and emits the current normalized public-run manifest table without downloading underlying files.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts exactly one provider-qualified archive run accession, for example `ena:ERR123456`. Local files and inline literals are rejected. The `--download` flag is parsed but explicitly rejected in v1 because direct acquisition is not yet part of the shipped surface.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The result is a stable table report describing the normalized manifest rows for the requested public run. For ENA-backed runs, v1 emits available FASTQ-oriented manifest rows. The method is currently manifest-reporting only and does not materialize files.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema runget`. Rust service coverage includes a mocked ENA manifest success path, explicit rejection of `--download`, and an explicit not-supported path for SRA manifest retrieval.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release is intentionally narrower than the historical EMBOSS name might suggest. `runget` does not yet download archive payloads, and SRA-backed manifest retrieval is not yet supported. The current curated evidence proves the governed manifest seam and its explicit limits rather than broad provider completeness.

artifacts.label: Mocked ENA runget case fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed case note for the mocked ENA manifest-report path used in Rust service coverage.

examples.title: Report an ENA public-run manifest without downloading files
examples.description: Resolves a provider-qualified ENA run accession and returns the normalized manifest table through the governed archive reporting path.

expected_outputs.label: Normalized ENA run manifest table
expected_outputs.description: A stable table report whose rows describe the available ENA FASTQ manifest entries for the requested run.
